page: Microsoft Azure D2_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4-series, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The server is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX512_VNNI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate that the D2_v4 delivers moderate single-core performance but struggles under multi-threaded and memory-intensive workloads. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test of 1427.09 and Geekbench single-core score of 1218.0, fall within the average range. Single-threaded gzip compression at 23.73 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.03 GB/sec also demonstrate stable single-core execution. However, multi-core and memory subsystem benchmarks are poor, placing in the bottom 10% of tested servers. This is reflected in the Geekbench multi-core score of 1466.0, a Passmark CPU score of 2218.71, and low memory bandwidth speeds, which reach only 12.96 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 10.78 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The primary tradeoff of the D2_v4 is its limited multi-threaded scaling and constrained memory bandwidth, which restrict its utility for high-throughput applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance represents an entry-level, low-density hardware profile suitable for basic utility workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power or intensive memory access. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, small-scale development or testing environments, simple text processing, and low-volume background tasks. It is not recommended for database operations, software compilation, or machine learning inference, where its benchmarked performance is weak.
description: Microsoft Azure D2_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a clear contrast: single-core performance is average, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1218.0, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Consequently, the D2_v4 is a qualitatively cost-efficient option for low-density, single-threaded utility tasks rather than resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight workloads like basic web serving, small-scale development environments, and simple background processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and no local storage. It offers average single-core performance for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suited for lightweight single-threaded utility workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors, balanced memory, and reliable single-core performance for basic utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz
Average single-core performance for basic computing tasks
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
